title: Unix socket permission hook for collaborative sessions
purpose: Reads input from stdin, forwards it to a Unix socket for permission validation, and outputs the response.
module_context: Sets up a Bun script that acts as a Unix socket client for permission checking in collaborative environments.

steps.content: Initialize a promise to collect all stdin data.
Accumulate incoming data chunks into a buffer.
Resolve the promise with the complete concatenated string once all data is received.
Reject the promise if an error occurs during data collection.
Establish a connection to the specified Unix socket.
Write the payload followed by a newline to the socket.
Accumulate incoming data chunks until a newline delimiter is found.
Resolve the promise with the response up to the first newline.
Close the socket connection after receiving the response.
Reject the promise if an error occurs during the socket operation.
Reject the promise if the socket closes before a response is received.
Retrieve the socket path from the environment variable.
IF the socket path is not set, write an error message and exit with code 2.
Validate that the input is valid JSON.
Send the input to the socket and await the response.
Write the response to stdout followed by a newline.
